question: What are the RSV setpoints according to Table 5.2-2?

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements, referencing specific ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) Code cases as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a and relevant Regulatory Guides. It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC conditions, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) is designed to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant's life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. The RCPB includes all pressure-retaining components of the reactor coolant system up to key isolation valves and safety systems, encompassing the reactor vessel, associated piping, valves, and connected systems. These design features ensure the RCPB's strength and reliability under all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details the use of ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) 2013 Edition for design, fabrication, and testing, and clarifies the classification of various piping and components according to their safety significance and regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases related to materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures for pressurized water reactors.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of alloys and stainless steels used, their compliance with ASME BPVC standards, and the purpose of each material for corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five, which covers the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It details that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the proprietary information within, subject to regulatory requirements, and mandates that all copies include the appropriate copyright and proprietary notices.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety valves, and associated piping and valves. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references where further design details can be found in other report sections.

edges.description: Section A references regulatory codes and standards that are necessary for the design and testing of overpressure protection systems, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary described in Section B.

Section A references the use of ASME BPVC code cases for design and construction, which are the same codes and standards that Section B states are complied with for the RPV and pressure-retaining components, connecting them through regulatory and code compliance.

Section A discusses regulatory code compliance for overpressure protection, while Section B lists references that may support or provide background for the technical and regulatory basis in Section A.

Section A describes the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that these features comply with regulatory codes and standards such as 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC.
Section A discusses the design features ensuring the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references that support the technical basis for those design features.

Section A describes compliance with regulatory codes for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B lists references that include the ASME code cited in Section A as the basis for compliance.

Section A describes the physical components and materials of the pressurizer, while Section B discusses regulatory compliance and code cases for the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, with both sections connected by their relevance to the design and regulatory requirements for pressurizer and reactor coolant system components.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of specific pressurizer components and their materials, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity is discussed in general terms in Section B.

Section A describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, some of which are referenced in Section B as being designed and constructed to meet the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III.

Section A describes the components of the reactor coolant system, including the reactor safety valves (RSVs), while Section B discusses the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that govern the design and overpressure protection requirements for those components, thus connecting the physical design (A) to the regulatory framework (B) that determines setpoints.

Section A lists the reactor safety valves (RSVs) as components within the reactor coolant system, while Section B defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to include pressure-retaining components such as the RSVs, connecting the identification of RSVs in A to their regulatory boundary context in B.

Section A describes the components of the reactor coolant system, including the RSVs whose setpoints are inquired about, while Section B establishes that these components (including RSVs) must comply with regulatory codes such as ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a, which may govern their setpoints.

Section A is the preface providing document context and copyright information, while Section B contains technical details about the reactor coolant system components, including RSVs, thus Section A serves as an introductory framework for the technical content found in Section B.
